data.body:
PASS-1 review note (criterion T11 — Data-migration / backfill safety): **Not applicable (PASS).**

T11 is an overlay that applies only when a plan changes a persisted schema/format or backfills data. This plan moves `.bridge_state/bindings.json` and the `.reconciler-*` lock/gate files OFF the shared `tickets` git branch by gitignoring them. It does not alter the `bindings.json` JSON schema, run any DDL, or backfill rows — `binding_store.py` keeps writing the same file at the same filesystem path (`.tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/bindings.json`); only its git-commit status on the shared branch changes. This is a storage-location / git-tracking change of an in-place file, which T11's anti-FP rule classes as not-applicable (no persisted-format migration, no batched backfill, no dual-write window over a schema change).

One adjacent observation that is NOT a T11 finding (it lands under T4/coordination-design, already flagged by the plan): existing deployed clones currently have `bindings.json` committed on the tickets branch, so a one-time `git rm --cached` + coordination-state transition is implied. The AC line "with reconciler coordination preserved or redesigned" and the Scope/risk note ("needs its own design pass") already acknowledge this, so it is not raised as a T11 gap.

No T11 findings.
